{"step_by_step_thinking": "Prevnar 13 is the brand name for PCV13, the 13-valent pneumococcal conjugate vaccine. Multiple documents confirm its use in children. Document [0] states that for children aged 6 weeks through 5 years, PCV13 is indicated for the prevention of invasive pneumococcal disease (IPD) and otitis media. Document [3] states that PCV13 and PCV15 can be used interchangeably for children aged 2 to 59 months. Document [8] specifies that unvaccinated children aged 24 to 71 months should receive a single dose of PCV13 or PCV15. Document [13] notes that in 2022, the FDA approved expanded indications for PCV15, including individuals aged 6 weeks through 17 years, and PCV13 and PCV15 can be used interchangeably. Document [16] states that PCV13 is not routinely recommended for healthy children above 5 years of age, but this does not negate its use in younger children. The minimum age for PCV13 is 6 weeks, as stated in Document [18]. Therefore, PCV13 is clearly indicated and used in children from 6 weeks of age through 17 years. The evidence consistently supports its use in children across various age groups, including infants, toddlers, and adolescents.", "answer_choice": "A"}
